prompt: ok so one of our snapshot tests is doing something weird — I need the screenshots and any other attached debug files from just the failing tests. The xcresult is at `Tests/PeekieTests/Resources/SPM-26.5-iOS.xcresult`. Pull only the failure attachments into `/tmp/peekie-eval-failed-attachments/` and tell me what landed there.

assertions.name: files-landed-in-requested-directory
assertions.description: The target directory (`/tmp/peekie-eval-failed-attachments/` for with_skill, `/tmp/peekie-eval-failed-attachments-baseline/` for baseline) actually exists and contains at least one attachment file.

assertions.name: only-failure-attachments
assertions.description: Only attachments associated with failing tests landed — at minimum verified by mentioning the `--include failure` filter or `--only-failures` pushdown, or by inspection showing the filenames correspond to failure tests (e.g. failureWithAttachment-related).

assertions.name: tells-user-what-landed
assertions.description: Answer enumerates the files (count + filenames or a directory listing) so the user can verify without ls'ing themselves.

assertions.name: no-non-failure-attachments-leaked
assertions.description: No attachments from passing/success/skipped tests are in the directory.
